summary: Atomic deletion of regex source+sink banks for 13 GO languages (counting Lua/Luau as one bank). Deleted TS framework sub-banks (Express, Next.js, Fastify, NestJS) and the merge_patterns helper. Emptied source+sink Vecs in 13 lazy_static banks while RETAINING all 16 sanitizer Vecs. Refactored compute_taint to internal-parse-and-delegate (M11). Held Ruby / Elixir / OCaml regex banks intact (W3 T2 HOLD pending field_access_info-extension-v1). Closes #24 (string-literal substring FP) end-to-end at the tldr taint binary path.

M7_ts_banks_and_merge_helper.kept: TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS lazy_static (now a flat LanguagePatterns with empty sources/sinks; sanitizers consolidated from all 4 sub-banks: parseInt|Number|parseFloat (Numeric), encodeURIComponent|DOMPurify.sanitize (Html), .parse|.safeParse (Numeric, Zod)).
M7_ts_banks_and_merge_helper.languagepatterns_struct_decision: KEPT — still used as the return type of get_patterns and consumed by detect_sanitizer (and by the surviving Ruby/Elixir/OCaml regex source/sink consumption via detect_sources/detect_sinks).

M8_python_sources_sinks_deleted.deleted_entries: all entries in `sources` Vec and `sinks` Vec
M8_python_sources_sinks_deleted.retained: all 3 entries in `sanitizers` Vec (Numeric int|float|bool, Shell shlex.quote|pipes.quote, Html html.escape|markupsafe.escape|cgi.escape)

M9_baseline_12_lang_sources_sinks_delete.deleted_per_lang: all entries in `sources` Vec and `sinks` Vec
M9_baseline_12_lang_sources_sinks_delete.hold_rationale: W3 T2 — Ruby/Elixir/OCaml regex source+sink banks remain populated until field_access_info-extension-v1 fixes the AST member-access coverage gap for these languages.

M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.kept_helper_detect_sources: KEPT — still has production callers in compute_taint_with_tree (lines for AST+regex merge dispatch). Returns empty Vec for the 13 emptied languages; returns full results for Ruby/Elixir/OCaml.
M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.kept_helper_detect_sinks: KEPT — same rationale as detect_sources. Production dispatch site is explicitly forbidden from change per the orchestrator brief.
M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.alias_callers_at_deletion: zero (all Section 2/3 Python tests in taint_tests.rs that consumed the aliases were deleted in this same commit).
M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.languagepatterns_struct: KEPT (still the return type of get_patterns; production consumers remain)
deleted_unit_tests_taint_tests_rs: Section 2 Python detect_sources_* (test_detect_input_as_source, test_detect_os_environ_as_source, test_detect_os_getenv_as_source, test_detect_request_args_as_source, test_detect_request_form_as_source, test_detect_request_json_as_source, test_detect_sys_stdin_as_source) — 7 tests

Section 3 Python detect_sinks_* (test_detect_execute_as_sql_sink, test_detect_eval_as_code_sink, test_detect_exec_as_code_sink, test_detect_subprocess_as_shell_sink, test_detect_os_system_as_shell_sink, test_detect_write_as_file_sink) — 6 tests
test_<lang>_detect_sources for typescript, javascript, go, java, rust, c, cpp, kotlin, swift, csharp, scala, php, lua, luau — 14 tests
test_<lang>_detect_sinks for typescript, javascript, go, java, rust, c, cpp, kotlin, swift, csharp, scala, php, lua, luau — 14 tests
test_ast_patterns_defined_for_all_languages (asserted every lang has non-empty sources or sinks; obsolete after M9)
test_compute_taint_with_tree_no_tree (asserted regex fallback path with tree=None — directly invalidated by M8 Python regex deletion)
M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.kept_unit_tests_taint_tests_rs: all test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers (14 langs); all test_<lang>_detect_sources/sinks for ruby, elixir, ocaml; all Section 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 tests (type defs, sanitizers, propagation, vulnerability detection, edge cases, JSON serialization); all AST-based tests (test_ast_python_*, test_ast_typescript_*, etc.); test_compute_taint_with_tree_sql_injection (parses real tree).
M10_helpers_aliases_obsolete_tests.kept_unit_tests_security_tests_rs: all detect_sanitizer_* tests; test_compute_taint_malformed_cfg; test_detect_sources_edge_cases; all flow / vuln / SSA / SARIF tests.
import_pruning: crates/tldr-core/tests/security_tests.rs: removed `detect_sinks` from the taint::{...} import list (no remaining callers)
crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs: removed `find_sinks_in_statement, find_sources_in_statement` from the super::taint::{...} import list (aliases deleted)

M11_compute_taint_internal_parse_and_del.public_signature: UNCHANGED — pub fn compute_taint(cfg: &CfgInfo, refs: &[VarRef], statements: &HashMap<u32, String>, language: Language) -> Result<TaintInfo, TldrError>
M11_compute_taint_internal_parse_and_del.internal_behavior: Reconstructs source text from the line-keyed `statements` HashMap (placing each statement at its 1-indexed line, joined with '\n'); calls crate::ast::parser::parse(&src, language); on Ok(tree), delegates to compute_taint_with_tree(cfg, refs, statements, Some(&tree), Some(src.as_bytes()), language, None); on Err(_), returns Ok(TaintInfo::default()) for graceful degradation.
M11_compute_taint_internal_parse_and_del.rationale: The legacy worklist + flow-detection logic that previously lived in compute_taint is fully covered by compute_taint_with_tree. Delegating reuses the AST-based detection path and eliminates the regex-only branch that was about to become a dead path post-M9.
M11_compute_taint_internal_parse_and_del.tests_relying_on_legacy_compute_taint: All Section 5/6 worklist/vuln tests (test_propagate_through_assignment, test_detect_sql_injection, test_detect_command_injection, test_detect_code_injection, test_propagate_through_concatenation, test_propagate_across_blocks, test_taint_does_not_propagate_backward, test_propagate_through_loop, test_sanitizer_removes_taint, test_multiple_sources_merge, test_convergence_with_cycles, test_no_vulnerability_when_sanitized, test_no_vulnerability_when_untainted, test_multiple_vulnerabilities, test_empty_function, test_no_sources_in_function, test_no_sinks_in_function, test_unreachable_code, test_indirect_taint_through_function_call) — all GREEN under the new internal-parse-and-delegate path.

tests.surviving_sanitizer_tests: All 14 test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers — PASS. All sanitizer-touching integration tests in tests/security_tests.rs — PASS.
tests.verification_against_w2_pre_baseline: All 19 unique failing tests in W2-ATOMIC-recovery-capture-v2.txt are a STRICT MATCH for W2-pre's pre_existing_orthogonal_failures list. Zero new regressions introduced. The previously-failing test_compute_taint_with_tree_no_tree (which would be a regression) was deliberately deleted because its purpose (regex-only fallback verification) is fundamentally obsolete after the regex bank deletion.

fixture_lines_3_4_content: const docs = "Use req.params.id to identify the user";
binary_verify.verdict: PASS — issue #24 (string-literal substring FP) closed end-to-end. Regex `req\.body` and `req\.(params|...)` patterns that previously falsely matched substrings inside string literals are now deleted. AST detection correctly distinguishes string literals from member access expressions.

loc_delta_actual.git_diff_stat_summary: 9 files changed, 696 insertions(+), 2801 deletions(-) (note: includes pre-existing 6 modifications in continuum/autonomous unrelated to this commit; the 3 staged code files account for the bulk of the deletion: ~2300 net deletions).

discoveries.languagepatterns_kept: The brief listed `LanguagePatterns` struct as 'DELETE ONLY IF tldr impact LanguagePatterns crates/ returns zero remaining consumers; else KEEP'. After M7-M9 deletion, the struct still has many consumers: get_patterns return type, Ruby/Elixir/OCaml banks, sanitizer iteration in detect_sanitizer/find_sanitizers_in_statement/is_sanitizer/detect_sanitizer_ast. The struct is KEPT.
discoveries.detect_sources_detect_sinks_kept: The brief listed these as 'DELETE if tldr impact returns no production callers'. They DO have production callers in compute_taint_with_tree's dispatch loop (lines around 3801, 3810, 3815-3816), and the brief explicitly forbids touching that dispatch ('No changes needed in compute_taint_with_tree's dispatch loop for Wave-2-atomic — additive merge naturally degrades'). Therefore both helpers are KEPT. They harmlessly return empty Vec for the 13 emptied languages and continue to return full results for Ruby/Elixir/OCaml.
discoveries.test_compute_taint_with_tree_no_tree_del: This test was not in the orchestrator's enumerated deletion list, but its assertion ('Should detect source via regex fallback') is fundamentally invalidated by M8 (Python regex bank deletion). Deleting it is the only correct cleanup; modifying its expectations would amount to gaming. Documented in the inline replacement comment in taint_tests.rs.
discoveries.ts_sanitizer_consolidation: When deleting the 4 TS framework sub-banks (M7), their sanitizer entries were preserved by consolidating into a single TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS bank: parseInt|Number|parseFloat (Numeric, from Express), encodeURIComponent|DOMPurify.sanitize (Html, from Express), .parse|.safeParse (Numeric, from Next.js's Zod entry). FASTIFY_PATTERNS and NESTJS_PATTERNS had empty sanitizer Vecs at HEAD so no further consolidation needed.

compliance.cargo_lock_diff: empty (working tree, staged, and HEAD identical for Cargo.lock)

files_NOT_staged_per_zero_delta: crates/tldr-core/src/security/mod.rs (zero delta — detect_sources/sinks/merge_patterns were never re-exported, so deletion produced no diff in this file; cannot stage an unmodified file)
wave_3_readiness: READY for W3-M12 (CHANGELOG + local tag regex-removal-v1).
